architecture.summary: GLR is an LLM-based knowledge graph completion framework that combines three mechanisms: (1) Graph Chain-of-Thought reasoning, which injects local subgraph structures into prompts to guide step-wise multi-hop relational reasoning; (2) LoRA fine-tuning of the LLM for efficient KGC-specific adaptation; and (3) a P(True)-based confidence ranking mechanism that scores candidate entities by their predicted probability of forming true triples. The flow is: prompt with graph-constrained CoT → LLM generates reasoning + candidate → confidence scoring → ranked entity output.

biblio.title: GLR: Graph Chain-of-Thought with LoRA Fine-Tuning and Confidence Ranking for Knowledge Graph Completion

claims.statement: GLR achieves a Mean Reciprocal Rank (MRR) of 0.507 on FB15K-237, marking a 6.8% improvement over the best recent instruction-tuned method, DIFT combined with CoLE
Graph-CoT introduces local subgraph structures to guide LLMs in performing graph-constrained, step-wise reasoning, improving their ability to model multi-hop relational patterns
LoRA-based fine-tuning enables efficient adaptation of LLMs to the KGC scenario with minimal computational overhead, further enhancing the model's capability for graph-structured reasoning
The P(True) mechanism quantifies the reliability of candidate entities, improving the robustness of ranking and the controllability of outputs

GLR maintains significant performance advantages on WN18RR and UMLS, verifying its effectiveness in enhancing both the structured reasoning capabilities and the prediction reliability of LLMs for KGC tasks
